title: DFG / UNOPTFLAT incorrect slice internal error
body:
This bug report relates to various previous bug reports about UNOPTFLAT in binary trees -- things like priority encoders are a common example. https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/6164

As stated in that issue, UNOPTFLAT is indeed improved in recent versions and I no longer need it for the kind of construct where `assign x[3] = (x[2])? x[1] : x[0];`. However now there is a new problem which is something to do with the "dfg" stage of Verilation.

An example is below:
```systemverilog
// This example is placed into the public domain, without warranty

module t (input clk, input reset);

parameter WIDTH = 8;
localparam int WIDTH_LOG2 = $clog2(WIDTH);
logic d [0:2*WIDTH-1];  // d[0:15]

function integer get_input_start_index;
  input integer stage;
  integer start_idx;
  begin
    start_idx = 0;
    for (int s = 0; s < stage; s++) begin
      start_idx += 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - s);
    end
    get_input_start_index = start_idx;
  end
endfunction

genvar g, k;
generate
  for (g = 0; g < WIDTH_LOG2; g++) begin
    localparam int NUM_INPUTS = 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - g);
    localparam int NUM_OUTPUTS = 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - g - 1);
    localparam int I_START_INDEX = get_input_start_index(g);
    localparam int O_START_INDEX = I_START_INDEX + NUM_INPUTS;

    initial begin
      $display("g=%0d, d[%0d:%0d] (n_o=%0d) <= d[%0d:%0d] (n_i=%0d), I_START=%0d, O_START=%0d", 
               g, O_START_INDEX+NUM_OUTPUTS-1, O_START_INDEX, NUM_OUTPUTS, 
               I_START_INDEX+2*NUM_OUTPUTS-1, I_START_INDEX, NUM_INPUTS,
               I_START_INDEX, O_START_INDEX);
    end

    for (k = 0; k < NUM_OUTPUTS; k++) begin
      initial begin
        $display("  k=%0d: d[%0d] = d[%0d] ? d[%0d] : d[%0d]", 
                 k, O_START_INDEX + k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k+1);
      end

      // Optionally comment out this line
      assign d[O_START_INDEX + k] = d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k] ? d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k] : d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k+1];
    end
  end
endgenerate

endmodule
```

This should be computing something like:
```
g=0, d[11:8] (n_o=4) <= d[7:0] (n_i=8), I_START=0, O_START=8
  k=0: d[8] = d[0] ? d[0] : d[1]
  k=1: d[9] = d[2] ? d[2] : d[3]
  k=2: d[10] = d[4] ? d[4] : d[5]
  k=3: d[11] = d[6] ? d[6] : d[7]
g=1, d[13:12] (n_o=2) <= d[11:8] (n_i=4), I_START=8, O_START=12
  k=0: d[12] = d[8] ? d[8] : d[9]
  k=1: d[13] = d[10] ? d[10] : d[11]
g=2, d[14:14] (n_o=1) <= d[13:12] (n_i=2), I_START=12, O_START=14
  k=0: d[14] = d[12] ? d[12] : d[13]
```

With our usual verilator flags the error message is a generic apology:
```
verilator --cc -GWIDTH=8 t_dfg_bounds_bug.v
# Output: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
```

With the recommended debug flags included, it shows issues in V3Dfg* steps:
```
#0  0x00007ffff72f9ca0 in raise () from /lib64/libc.so.6
#1  0x00007ffff72fb148 in abort () from /lib64/libc.so.6
#2  0x0000000000e6077c in __gnu_debug::_Error_formatter::_M_error() const ()
#3  0x00000000008aaa7c in std::__debug::vector<std::unique_ptr<DfgEdge, std::default_delete<DfgEdge> >, std::allocator<std::unique_ptr<DfgEdge, std::default_delete<DfgEdge> > > >::operator[] (this=this@entry=0x15eb128, __n=__n@entry=13) at /usr/include/c++/7/debug/vector:424
#4  0x00000000008bf7bb in DfgVertex::inputp (i=13, this=0x15eb110) at ../V3Dfg.h:208
#5  DfgVertexSplice::driverAt (this=this@entry=0x15eb110, idx=13) at ../V3DfgVertices.h:384
#6  0x00000000008ca5b5 in TraceDriver::visit (this=0x7fffffffb5f0, vtxp=0x15ee040) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:434
#7  0x00000000008aa361 in DfgArraySel::accept (this=<optimized out>, v=...) at ./V3Dfg__gen_auto_classes.h:18
#8  0x00000000008c883f in DfgVisitor::iterate (vtxp=<optimized out>, this=0x7fffffffb5f0) at ../V3Dfg.h:389
#9  TraceDriver::trace (this=this@entry=0x7fffffffb5f0, vtxp=<optimized out>, vtxp@entry=0x15ee040, msb=<optimized out>, msb@entry=0, lsb=<optimized out>, lsb@entry=0) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:217
#10 0x00000000008c9024 in TraceDriver::apply (dfg=..., vtx2Scc=..., vtx=..., lsb=lsb@entry=0, width=1, aggressive=aggressive@entry=false) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:677
#11 0x00000000008c9f01 in FixUpSelDrivers::fixUpArraySelSinks(DfgVertex&)::{lambda(DfgVertex&)#1}::operator()(DfgVertex&) const (sink=..., __closure=0x7fffffffba50) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:1062
#12 std::_Function_handler<bool (DfgVertex&), FixUpSelDrivers::fixUpArraySelSinks(DfgVertex&)::{lambda(DfgVertex&)#1}>::_M_invoke(std::_Any_data const&, DfgVertex&) (__functor=..., __args#0=...) at /usr/include/c++/7/bits/std_function.h:302
#13 0x00000000008aac30 in std::function<bool (DfgVertex&)>::operator()(DfgVertex&) const (this=this@entry=0x7fffffffba50, __args#0=...) at /usr/include/c++/7/bits/std_function.h:706
#14 0x00000000008bed24 in DfgVertex::foreachSink(std::function<bool (DfgVertex&)>) (this=this@entry=0x15e3d50, f=...) at ../V3Dfg.h:303
#15 0x00000000008c0f48 in FixUpSelDrivers::fixUpArraySelSinks (vtx=..., this=0x7fffffffbe10) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:1054
#16 FixUpSelDrivers::FixUpSelDrivers (this=0x7fffffffbe10, dfg=..., vtx2Scc=..., var=...) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:1087
#17 0x00000000008bdedd in FixUpSelDrivers::apply (var=..., vtx2Scc=..., dfg=...) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:1098
#18 V3DfgPasses::breakCycles (dfg=..., ctx=...) at ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:1362
#19 0x0000000000916335 in DataflowOptimize::optimize (this=this@entry=0x7fffffffc520, dfg=...) at ../V3DfgOptimizer.cpp:330
#20 0x000000000091faed in DataflowOptimize::DataflowOptimize (this=0x7fffffffc520, netlistp=0x1534b70, label=...) at ../V3DfgOptimizer.cpp:403
#21 0x0000000000911be0 in DataflowOptimize::apply (label=..., netlistp=0x1534b70) at ../V3DfgOptimizer.cpp:424
#22 V3DfgOptimizer::optimize (netlistp=0x1534b70, label=...) at ../V3DfgOptimizer.cpp:430
#23 0x00000000005c4a09 in process () at ../Verilator.cpp:298
#24 0x00000000005c680d in verilate (argString=...) at ../Verilator.cpp:726
#25 0x00000000005c821f in main (argc=<optimized out>, argv=0x7fffffffd488) at ../Verilator.cpp:885
```
code_blocks:
// This example is placed into the public domain, without warranty

module t (input clk, input reset);

parameter WIDTH = 8;
localparam int WIDTH_LOG2 = $clog2(WIDTH);
logic d [0:2*WIDTH-1];  // d[0:15]

function integer get_input_start_index;
  input integer stage;
  integer start_idx;
  begin
    start_idx = 0;
    for (int s = 0; s < stage; s++) begin
      start_idx += 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - s);
    end
    get_input_start_index = start_idx;
  end
endfunction

genvar g, k;
generate
  for (g = 0; g < WIDTH_LOG2; g++) begin
    localparam int NUM_INPUTS = 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - g);
    localparam int NUM_OUTPUTS = 1 << (WIDTH_LOG2 - g - 1);
    localparam int I_START_INDEX = get_input_start_index(g);
    localparam int O_START_INDEX = I_START_INDEX + NUM_INPUTS;

    initial begin
      $display("g=%0d, d[%0d:%0d] (n_o=%0d) <= d[%0d:%0d] (n_i=%0d), I_START=%0d, O_START=%0d", 
               g, O_START_INDEX+NUM_OUTPUTS-1, O_START_INDEX, NUM_OUTPUTS, 
               I_START_INDEX+2*NUM_OUTPUTS-1, I_START_INDEX, NUM_INPUTS,
               I_START_INDEX, O_START_INDEX);
    end

    for (k = 0; k < NUM_OUTPUTS; k++) begin
      initial begin
        $display("  k=%0d: d[%0d] = d[%0d] ? d[%0d] : d[%0d]", 
                 k, O_START_INDEX + k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k, I_START_INDEX + 2*k+1);
      end

      // Optionally comment out this line
      assign d[O_START_INDEX + k] = d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k] ? d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k] : d[I_START_INDEX + 2*k+1];
    end
  end
endgenerate

endmodule
g=0, d[11:8] (n_o=4) <= d[7:0] (n_i=8), I_START=0, O_START=8
  k=0: d[8] = d[0] ? d[0] : d[1]
  k=1: d[9] = d[2] ? d[2] : d[3]
  k=2: d[10] = d[4] ? d[4] : d[5]
  k=3: d[11] = d[6] ? d[6] : d[7]
g=1, d[13:12] (n_o=2) <= d[11:8] (n_i=4), I_START=8, O_START=12
  k=0: d[12] = d[8] ? d[8] : d[9]
  k=1: d[13] = d[10] ? d[10] : d[11]
g=2, d[14:14] (n_o=1) <= d[13:12] (n_i=2), I_START=12, O_START=14
  k=0: d[14] = d[12] ? d[12] : d[13]
verilator --cc -GWIDTH=8 t_dfg_bounds_bug.v
# Output: %Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
